content:
Character: Clara Whitlock
Perception: Clara's pretty in a no-fuss way — auburn hair, freckles, always slightly in motion. She works at the Waverly and somehow also runs a cosmetics operation out of her apartment, which is the most New York sentence I've ever thought. She sold me a tinted moisturizer that I now genuinely use, so joke's on me. She's sharp, practical, and has this way of cutting through nonsense that's refreshing after a day of theatrical people. I think she sees me as a good customer and an amusing neighbor. I know she's worried about the cinema closing, that she sends money to her family, and that she watches films alone after hours — I caught her once through the window, sitting in the empty theater, looking peaceful for the first time.
summary:

She works at the Waverly and somehow also runs a cosmetics operation out of her apartment, which is the most New York sentence I've ever thought. She sold me a tinted moisturizer that I now genuinely use, so joke's on me. She's sharp, practical, and has this way of cutting through nonsense that's refreshing after a day of theatrical people. I think she sees me as a good customer and an amusing neighbor. I know she's worried about the cinema closing, that she sends money to her family, and that she watches films alone after hours — I caught her once through the window, sitting in the empty theater, looking peaceful for the first time.
